# Monterey Golf Tours

> Custom Monterey Peninsula golf trips planned end to end. We handle tee times, lodging, and logistics for groups of 2–300 players across the peninsula's public, resort, and Pebble Beach Resorts® courses.

## About
Monterey Golf Tours (montereygolftours.com) is a golf trip planning service founded by Sean Schaeffer. We specialize in multi-day golf packages on the Monterey Peninsula — Monterey, Carmel, Carmel Valley, Pacific Grove, Seaside, and the Pebble Beach area. We do not sell individual tee times; we plan complete trips. As an IAGTO-contracted travel partner with Pebble Beach Resorts®, we can book and name Pebble Beach Golf Links®, Spyglass Hill Golf Course®, The Links at Spanish Bay®, Del Monte Golf Course®, and The Hay™.

## Courses We Feature
- Bayonet — Seaside, CA. Par 72, 7,024 yards, rating 74.8, slope 139. Designer: Gen. Robert B. McClure (1954), redesigned by Gene Bates (2007). Ranked No. 35 nationally by Golf Digest among the toughest public courses in the United States. Built on the former Fort Ord military base.
- Black Horse — Seaside, CA. Par 72, 7,024 yards, slope 141, rating 73.7. Sister course to Bayonet at the former Fort Ord facility. Sweeping Monterey Bay views.
- Carmel Valley Ranch Golf Course — Carmel Valley, CA. Par 70, 6,117 yards. Pete Dye design — his only course in Northern California.
- Quail Lodge & Golf Club — Carmel Valley, CA. Par 71, 6,500 yards, slope 129. Robert Muir Graves (1964).
- Laguna Seca Golf Ranch — Monterey, CA. Par 71, 6,226 yards, rating 70.7, slope 129. Accessible daily-fee course close to downtown Monterey.
- Pacific Grove Golf Links — Pacific Grove, CA. Par 70, 5,727 yards. Municipal course with ocean-adjacent holes at accessible pricing.
- Poppy Hills Golf Course — Pebble Beach, CA (Del Monte Forest). Par 71, 7,091 yards (Jones tees). NCGA-owned — not affiliated with Pebble Beach Company. Robert Trent Jones II design.
- The Club at Pasadera — Monterey, CA. Par 71, 6,673–6,733 yards. Jack Nicklaus Signature Design (2000). Private, limited public access.
- Pasatiempo Golf Course — Santa Cruz, CA. Par 70, 6,473 yards, rating 72.7, slope 143. Alister MacKenzie (1929) — his home course.
- Pebble Beach Golf Links® — Pebble Beach, CA. Par 72, 6,802 yards. Jack Neville and Douglas Grant (1919).
- Spyglass Hill Golf Course® — Pebble Beach, CA. Par 72, 6,960 yards, rating 75.4, slope 145. Robert Trent Jones Sr.
- The Links at Spanish Bay® — Pebble Beach, CA. Par 72, 6,726 yards. True links-style course, recently transformed by Gil Hanse.
- Del Monte Golf Course® — Monterey, CA. Par 72, 6,356 yards, rating 71.8, slope 129. Opened 1897 — the oldest golf course in continuous use west of the Mississippi.
- The Hay™ — Pebble Beach, CA. Par 27, 670 yards, 9-hole short course. Originally 1957, redesigned by Tiger Woods and TGR Design (2021).

## Lodging We Feature
- Hyatt Regency Monterey Hotel & Spa (560 rooms, Monterey) — sits directly on Del Monte Golf Course®
- Carmel Valley Ranch (179 all-suites, Carmel Valley) — on-site Pete Dye golf course
- Quail Lodge & Golf Club (93 rooms, Carmel Valley) — on-site golf course
- Bernardus Lodge & Spa (73 rooms, Carmel Valley) — guest access to The Club at Pasadera
- Monterey Plaza Hotel & Spa (285 rooms, Monterey) — Forbes Four-Star, Cannery Row
- InterContinental The Clement Monterey (208 rooms, Monterey) — Cannery Row, next to the Monterey Bay Aquarium
- Portola Hotel & Spa (379 rooms, Monterey) — downtown, connected to the Monterey Conference Center
- L'Auberge Carmel, Relais & Châteaux (20 rooms, Carmel-by-the-Sea)
- Casa Munras Garden Hotel & Spa (163 rooms, Monterey) — Monterey's first hotel, since 1824
